tools.name: messaging_auth_test
tools.description: Verify that a messaging platform is connected and show account info.

platform.description: Platform to test (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_list_conversations
tools.description: List channels, inboxes, DMs, or groups with unread counts.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

limit.description: Maximum number of conversations to return (default 200)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_read
tools.description: Read message history from a conversation. Optionally read a specific thread.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

thread_id.description: Thread ID to read replies from (optional)

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default 50)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_search
tools.description: Search messages across a platform using its native query syntax.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

query.description: Search query using the platform's native syntax

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 20)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_send
tools.description: Compose a message. For Gmail, creates a draft for review; for other platforms, sends directly. High-risk action. Include a confidence score (0-1).

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

conversation_id.description: Channel ID, email address, or conversation ID to send to

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_reply
tools.description: Reply in a thread. For Gmail, creates a threaded draft with reply-all recipients; for other platforms, sends directly. Include a confidence score (0-1).

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_mark_read
tools.description: Mark a conversation or message as read.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

message_id.description: Specific message ID to mark as read (optional)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_analyze_activity
tools.description: Analyze channel or conversation activity levels. Groups conversations as high/medium/low/dead activity.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_analyze_style
tools.description: Analyze sent messages to extract personal writing style patterns. Saves style characteristics to memory for personalized drafting. Run once to enable style-aware message composition.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "slack", "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

max_messages.description: Max sent messages to analyze (default 50, max 100)

query_filter.description: Optional search filter (e.g. 'to:alice@example.com' for Gmail, 'from:@me' for Slack)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_draft
tools.description: Create, list, or delete local drafts for any platform.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_archive
tools.description: Archive a Gmail message (remove from inbox). Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_batch_archive
tools.description: Archive multiple Gmail messages at once. Prefer scan_id + sender_ids (from a prior scan) over raw message_ids. Include a confidence score (0-1).

scan_id.description: Scan result ID from a prior gmail_sender_digest or gmail_outreach_scan call

sender_ids.description: Sender IDs to archive (used with scan_id to resolve message IDs server-side)

message_ids.description: Gmail message IDs to archive (fallback — prefer scan_id + sender_ids)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_archive_by_query
tools.description: Archive ALL Gmail messages matching a search query. Paginates through all results and archives in bulk. Ideal for high-volume senders where message IDs are not pre-collected. Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_label
tools.description: Add or remove labels on a Gmail message. Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_batch_label
tools.description: Add or remove labels on multiple Gmail messages. Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_trash
tools.description: Move a Gmail message to trash. Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_unsubscribe
tools.description: Unsubscribe from a mailing list by following the List-Unsubscribe header. Include a confidence score (0-1).

message_id.description: A Gmail message ID from the mailing list to unsubscribe from

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_draft
tools.description: Create a draft email in Gmail. The draft will appear in Gmail Drafts for user review.

in_reply_to.description: RFC 822 Message-ID header value (e.g. `<CABx...@mail.gmail.com>`), NOT the Gmail message ID. Look up the original message's Message-ID header.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_send_draft
tools.description: Send an existing Gmail draft. Only use when the user has reviewed and explicitly approved sending.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.description: List attachments on a Gmail message with filename, MIME type, size, and attachment ID.

tools.name: gmail_download_attachment
tools.description: Download a Gmail attachment to the working directory.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_send_with_attachments
tools.description: Create a Gmail draft with file attachments for review. Include a confidence score (0-1).

attachment_paths.description: Absolute file paths of attachments to include

thread_id.description: Thread ID to continue an existing thread

tools.description: Create a draft forwarding a Gmail message to another recipient, preserving attachments. Include a confidence score (0-1).

text.description: Optional text to prepend to the forwarded message

tools.description: Summarize a Gmail thread using LLM analysis. Returns participants, key decisions, open questions, and sentiment.

tools.name: gmail_follow_up
tools.description: Track, list, or untrack Gmail messages for follow-up using a dedicated label. Include a confidence score (0-1).

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_triage
tools.description: LLM-powered inbox triage: classify unread emails by urgency and type, optionally auto-apply actions. Include a confidence score (0-1).

auto_apply.description: Auto-archive can_archive and label needs_reply as Follow-up (default false)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_filters
tools.description: List, create, or delete Gmail filters for inbox automation. Include a confidence score (0-1).

add_label_ids.description: Filter action: label IDs to add (for create)

remove_label_ids.description: Filter action: label IDs to remove (for create)

forward.description: Filter action: forward to email (for create)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_vacation
tools.description: Get, enable, or disable Gmail vacation auto-responder. Include a confidence score (0-1).

restrict_to_domain.description: Only auto-reply to same domain (default false)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_sender_digest
tools.description: Scan Gmail and group messages by sender to identify high-volume senders (e.g. newsletters). Returns top senders sorted by message count with metadata for bulk cleanup.

page_token.description: Resume token from a previous scan (rarely needed — scans now cover up to 10,000 messages in a single call)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_sender_digest
tools.description: Scan connected email platform and group messages by sender to identify high-volume senders (e.g. newsletters). Works with any email provider that supports sender digest. Returns top senders sorted by message count with metadata for bulk cleanup.

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

page_token.description: Resume token from a previous scan (rarely needed — scans now cover up to 5,000 messages in a single call)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: messaging_archive_by_sender
tools.description: Archive all messages matching a search query on the connected email platform. Paginates through all results and archives in bulk. Works with any email provider that supports archive by query. Include a confidence score (0-1).

platform.description: Platform (e.g. "gmail"). Auto-detected if only one is connected.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: gmail_outreach_scan
tools.description: Scan Gmail for cold outreach emails (sales, recruiting, marketing) using LLM classification. Returns top outreach senders with suggested cleanup actions. Read-only — use gmail_batch_archive and gmail_filters for cleanup.

page_token.description: Resume token from a previous scan (rarely needed — scans now cover up to 5,000 messages in a single call)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: google_contacts
tools.description: List or search Google Contacts. Requires contacts.readonly scope (re-authorization may be needed).

page_size.description: Number of contacts to return (default 50, for list)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: sequence_create
tools.description: Create an email sequence with multiple steps and delay intervals.

delay_seconds.description: Delay before sending this step (in seconds)

body_prompt.description: Prompt or body text for this step

reply_to_thread.description: Whether to reply in the existing thread (default true for step 2+)

require_approval.description: Whether to require user approval before sending (default false)

exit_on_reply.description: Whether to stop the sequence when the contact replies (default true)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: sequence_list
tools.description: List all email sequences with their status and enrollment counts.

tools.name: sequence_get
tools.description: Get detailed information about a sequence including steps, enrollment counts, and status breakdown.

tools.name: sequence_update
tools.description: Update a sequence's name, description, status, steps, or exit-on-reply setting.

exit_on_reply.description: Whether to exit on contact reply

delay_seconds.description: Delay before sending this step (in seconds)

body_prompt.description: Prompt or body text for this step

reply_to_thread.description: Whether to reply in the existing thread

require_approval.description: Whether to require user approval before sending

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: sequence_delete
tools.description: Delete a sequence and cancel all its active enrollments.

tools.description: Enroll one or more contacts into a sequence. Accepts comma-separated emails or an array.

context.description: Optional context object with personalization variables for the enrolled contacts

tools.name: sequence_enrollment_list
tools.description: List enrollments for a sequence, optionally filtered by status.

tools.name: sequence_pause
tools.description: Pause a sequence (stops processing all enrollments) or cancel a specific enrollment.

tools.name: sequence_resume
tools.description: Resume a paused sequence. Active enrollments will be processed on the next scheduler tick.

tools.name: sequence_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a specific enrollment, removing the contact from the sequence.

tools.name: sequence_import
tools.description: Import contacts from a CSV/TSV file and enroll them in a sequence. First call shows a preview; set auto_enroll=true to enroll.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the CSV/TSV file

tools.name: sequence_analytics
tools.description: View sequence analytics dashboard with send/reply/completion metrics, per-step funnel, and recent activity.
